items.title: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly over ads as GLP-1 market hits ‘inflection point’
items.description: The Danish company Novo says U.S.-based Lilly isn’t including the most up-to-date clinical information about its drugs.

items.title: It’s getting easier for terminally ill people to end their own lives. What to know as laws change.
items.description: Patients must meet three conditions to qualify for Medical Aid in Dying.

items.title: Your healthcare could now cost $185,500 in retirement — and that doesn’t include long-term care
items.description: Rising prices for care and managing chronic conditions helped drive up the forecast.

items.title: Alphabet is pouring record cash into data centers, and earnings will show whether that’s paying off
items.description: Investors will be looking for more signs of AI monetization when Alphabet reports its earnings Wednesday.

items.title: GM’s stock bounces back as revenue grows for the first time in over a year
items.description: GM’s stock rises after a beat-and-raise earnings report, as revenue grows to snap a four-quarter streak of declines.

items.title: Why are gasoline prices rising faster than oil prices? Blame it on the ‘crack.’
items.description: The spread between the cost of barrels of gasoline and crude oil — known as the “crack spread” — has widened sharply.

items.title: The co-founder of Stocktwits dumped chip stocks before their 20% slide. Where he’s putting his money now.
items.description: Stocktwits’ Howard Lindzon talks to MarketWatch about sidestepping a chip selloff, where he sees opportunity and the “degenerate economy.”

items.title: Do I use $300,000 of my $1.2 million retirement savings so my daughter can attend her dream college?
items.description: “Our daughter is a highly ranked competitive softball pitcher with a 3.94 GPA.”

items.title: China has been buying the gold dip, and these analysts say a comeback is brewing
items.description: For the last few years, China’s buying patterns have been an excellent investment signal on gold

items.title: We have $8 million in traditional IRAs. Should we tap them to buy a house — and take the tax hit?
items.description: “We would rather pay taxes upfront than pay mortgage interest.”
